design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based menu-driven combat with party switching between two distinct groups (Aqutallions and Kustera); exploration to locate temples for rank advancement and recruit secondary party members
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Progression gated behind mandatory temple trials that grant Banalet rank status; dual-party system creates asymmetric resource constraints and forces tactical party selection
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a band of young Aqutallion warriors from lowly Pennon rank to legendary Banalet status; rebuild civilization by establishing a town and recruiting allies; culminate in defeating the world-threatening evil Homncruse
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits to a lightweight narrative-driven RPG with comedic villain personality ranges (from 'Cat Boo' to serious generals); explicitly balances accessibility for beginners with epic-length challenge for veterans; mandatory party-switching mechanic forces players to engage with full roster rather than optimize single team

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Party composition choice (Aqutallions vs. recruited Kustera) provides some tactical difficulty scaling; progression requires completing specific temple trials in sequence, reducing total flexibility

progression.upgrade_system: Rank advancement through temple trials grants Banalet status and secondary powers; recruited Kustera members (11 available) supplement primary party with temporary combat participation
progression.unlock_mechanism: Temple locations hidden around world; each temple grants rank advancement to one Aqutallion and provides Kustera-exclusive warp access to treasure dungeons; Aqutallion reunion unlocks Homncruse endgame
replayability_hooks: Eleven recruitable Kustera members enable different party configurations
Monster variety noted as collectible draw
Kustera-exclusive treasure dungeons incentivize secondary playthroughs with different recruitment strategies

world_building_techniques: Character arc: Five Aqutallion protagonists progress from Pennon rank through temple trials to Banalet union
Environmental storytelling: Hidden temples scattered across world serve as rank progression anchors
Town-building mechanic: Players establish refuge for Homncruse victims, grounding narrative stakes
Antagonist personality differentiation: Villains range from comedic (Cat Boo, Badbad) to elite generals, creating tonal variety

boss_design.philosophy: Encounter variety through both comedic and serious antagonist archetypes; temple trial bosses serve as rank gates; elite generals of Homncruse represent escalating threat intensity

faction_indicators: Aqutallion warriors (five player characters seeking to achieve Banalet rank)
danger_signals: Homncruse evil explicitly threatens world and creates refugee victims
Temple trials function as combat gauntlets protecting rank advancement
environmental_storytelling: Player-built town represents civilization restoration against Homncruse devastation
Temple locations function as pilgrimage sites granting warrior rank elevation
Kustera-exclusive treasure dungeons suggest secondary cultural or martial traditions
